description: Mapping of SecuriSphere correlation rules to MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs. Covers the 6 core Containers techniques (T1110, T1078, T1021, T1068, T1046, T1041) plus extended coverage (T1570 lateral movement, T1548 privilege escalation, T1526 cloud service discovery).

T1046.name: Network Service Discovery
T1046.description: Adversaries attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts and local network infrastructure devices, including those that may be vulnerable to remote software exploitation.

T1078.name: Valid Accounts
T1078.tactic: Initial Access / Persistence / Privilege Escalation / Defense Evasion
T1078.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts as a means of gaining Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, or Defense Evasion.

T1021.name: Remote Services
T1021.description: Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into a service that accepts remote connections, such as telnet, SSH, and VNC.

T1068.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
T1068.description: Adversaries may exploit software vulnerabilities in an attempt to elevate privileges. Exploitation of a software vulnerability occurs when an adversary takes advantage of a programming error.

T1110.name: Brute Force
T1110.description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts when passwords are unknown or when password hashes are obtained.

T1110.004.name: Credential Stuffing
T1110.004.description: Adversaries may use credentials obtained from breach dumps of unrelated accounts to gain access to target accounts through credential overlap.

T1041.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
T1041.description: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over an existing command and control channel.

T1570.name: Lateral Tool Transfer
T1570.description: Adversaries may transfer tools or other files between systems in a compromised environment.

T1548.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
T1548.description: Adversaries may circumvent mechanisms designed to control elevate privileges to gain higher-level permissions.

T1526.name: Cloud Service Discovery
T1526.description: An adversary may attempt to enumerate the cloud services running on a system after gaining access.

T1190.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
T1190.description: Adversaries may attempt to exploit a weakness in an Internet-facing host or system to initially access a network.

T1595.name: Active Scanning
T1595.description: Adversaries may execute active reconnaissance scans to gather information that can be used during targeting.

T1003.name: OS Credential Dumping
T1003.description: Adversaries may attempt to dump credentials to obtain account login and credential material.

T1071.name: Application Layer Protocol
T1071.description: Adversaries may communicate using OSI application layer protocols to avoid detection by blending in with existing traffic.

T1530.name: Data from Cloud Storage
T1530.description: Adversaries may access data objects from improperly secured cloud storage.

T1083.name: File and Directory Discovery
T1083.description: Adversaries may enumerate files and directories to find information about the environment.

T1048.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
T1048.description: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over a different protocol than that of the existing C2 channel.

recon_to_exploit.description: Port scan followed by SQL injection / path traversal from same source IP within 10 minutes

credential_compromise.description: Successful login after brute force / credential stuffing against same account

full_kill_chain.description: Multi-vector campaign crossing network, api, and auth layers from same IP

api_auth_combined.description: Source IP hitting API and Auth endpoints simultaneously (automated tooling)

distributed_attack.description: Same account targeted from 3+ distinct IPs (credential stuffing from botnet)

data_exfiltration.description: Sensitive data endpoint accessed after SQL injection / path traversal

persistent_threat.description: 10+ events over 5+ minutes with 3+ distinct attack types from same IP

brute_force_attempt.description: Single brute force or credential stuffing event

critical_exploit_attempt.description: 2+ critical exploit attempts (SQLi / XSS / path traversal) from same IP

browser_sqli.description: Browser-layer SQL injection payload detected by client-side agent

browser_brute_force.description: 5+ auth_failure events from same site_id within 60 seconds

browser_recon_scan.description: 10+ distinct target entities probed within 30 seconds

browser_bruteforce_to_exfil.description: Kill chain A: 5+ auth failures followed by data_access within 5 minutes

browser_recon_to_privesc.description: Kill chain B: Recon scan of 10+ entities followed by privilege_change within 3 minutes

browser_multi_hop.description: Kill chain C: 3+ distinct target entities hit in sequence within 2 minutes
